mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A breach of the 4.0 percent unemployment rate threshold signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce a positive Sharpe ratio on 60-day forward returns over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints that compress profit margins and force negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods yield positive 20-day forward returns in over 60 percent of cases, invalidating the supply-constraint channel.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above the 0.4 percent monthly threshold forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, immediately depressing equity valuations within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than half of the months where CPIAUCSL MoM growth surpasses 0.4 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls sustains aggregate consumption and corporate revenue expansion, producing reliably positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS growth against 60-day forward returns drops below 0.01, or the signal loses statistical significance at the 95 percent confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms robust economic momentum that lifts equity prices over the next month.
failure_condition: Concurrent positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to generate a positive mean 20-day forward return over a 10-year sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial output during a post-recession recovery phase signals capacity utilization improvements that boost near-term corporate earnings expectations and drive positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery phases are followed by negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of observations.
